name: web-ux-test
description: Workflow-enforced Playwright UX testing. The web-ux-test CLI owns workflow state, plan validation, Playwright execution, failure classification, and gated repair. A thin MCP server exposes high-level workflow tools.

interface.shortDescription: Workflow-enforced Playwright UX testing: schema-validated plans, gated execution, deterministic repair loop.
interface.longDescription: User-facing orchestrator for a strict, stateful Playwright UX testing workflow. The web-ux-test CLI is the authority: it owns the workflow state machine (14 phases), validates test-plan YAML against a JSON Schema, materializes plans into deterministic Playwright specs, executes them via the Playwright CLI, classifies failures, and gates repair proposals behind explicit approval. A thin MCP server exposes high-level workflow tools (create_test_plan, validate_test_plan, get_workflow_state, run_next_workflow_step, classify_latest_failure, propose_repair, approve_repair, apply_approved_repair, generate_report). State, runs, and reports persist under a project-local .web-ux-testing/ directory.
defaultPrompt: Initialize a web UX test project in this repository.
Create and validate a test plan for the dashboard login flow.
Classify the latest test failure and propose a repair.
Generate the report for the latest test run.
